steps.title: Identify the contrast signal
steps.content: The sentence opens with Although, a contrast signal. The second clause says recent analyses have sharpened our picture — meaning clarified it. The blank, which describes the earlier state, must therefore mean the opposite of sharp: vague or unclear.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice, then confirm the correct one
steps.content:
• "nebulous": Means hazy and unclear — this is the opposite of sharpened and fits the contrast set up by "Although" perfectly. ✓
  • "intricate": Means complex and detailed — intricate knowledge is already rich in content; it would not need to be sharpened into a clearer picture. ✗
  • "unprecedented": Means never seen before — this describes novelty, not a lack of clarity, so it doesn't contrast with sharpening. ✗
  • "concrete": Means solid and definite — concrete knowledge is already clear, which directly contradicts the need for recent analyses to sharpen it. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
